data_sources.name: NASA Technical Reports Server — MA-6 Mission Report
data_sources.description: Results of the First United States Manned Orbital Space Flight, February 20, 1962. The official post-flight report containing flight data, telemetry, pilot report, medical findings, and engineering analysis including the Segment 51 heat shield indicator investigation.
data_sources.relevance: Primary source for all mission data, pilot observations, and the heat shield telemetry anomaly

data_sources.name: NASA Space Science Data Coordinated Archive — Mercury-Atlas 6
data_sources.description: NSSDC master catalog entry for Friendship 7. Contains orbital parameters, spacecraft specifications, experiment descriptions, and links to archived telemetry data.
data_sources.relevance: Authoritative orbital element source: perigee 161 km, apogee 265 km, inclination 32.54°, period 88.48 min

data_sources.name: Project Mercury: A Chronology — NASA SP-4001
data_sources.description: James M. Grimwood's day-by-day chronology of Project Mercury from conception through the final flight. The MA-6 entries document the ten launch delays, the flight timeline, and the Segment 51 investigation.
data_sources.relevance: Definitive chronological source for the ten delays and engineering decisions

data_sources.name: This New Ocean: A History of Project Mercury — NASA SP-4201
data_sources.description: Swenson, Grimwood, and Alexander's comprehensive history of Project Mercury. Chapter 12 covers MA-6 in detail, including Chris Kraft's decision-making process during the Segment 51 crisis.
data_sources.relevance: Narrative engineering history with technical depth on flight control decisions

data_sources.name: StreamNet — Pacific Salmon Data
data_sources.description: Coordinated database of Pacific salmon population data across the Columbia River Basin. Contains spawning escapement counts, hatchery returns, harvest data, and habitat indicators for Chinook salmon (Oncorhynchus tshawytscha) by run (spring, summer, fall).
data_sources.relevance: Quantitative population data for the organism pairing — historical and current Chinook run sizes

data_sources.name: NOAA Fisheries — West Coast Chinook Salmon
data_sources.description: NOAA's species profile for Chinook salmon including ESA listing status by Evolutionarily Significant Unit (ESU), critical habitat designations, recovery plans, and population viability assessments.
data_sources.relevance: Conservation status and ESA listing data for the organism pairing

data_sources.name: Katherine Johnson Papers — Langley Research Center
data_sources.description: Documentation of Katherine Johnson's computational work at NASA Langley, including her verification of the IBM 7090 orbital trajectory calculations for Glenn's MA-6 flight. Glenn personally requested that Johnson check the computer's numbers before he would fly.
data_sources.relevance: Human computation and verification of orbital mechanics — the math that made the flight possible

data_sources.name: Mercury Tracking Network Documentation
data_sources.description: Technical documentation of the worldwide Mercury tracking network — 18 stations spanning the globe. Includes station coordinates, equipment specifications, communication links, and real-time data processing capabilities.
data_sources.relevance: Infrastructure data for the global tracking network that supported Glenn's orbital flight

data_sources.name: eBird — Swainson's Thrush Observations
data_sources.description: Cornell Lab of Ornithology's global bird observation database. Swainson's Thrush (Catharus ustulatus) sighting data including migration timing, breeding range, abundance maps, and vocalization recordings for the Pacific Northwest.
data_sources.relevance: Distribution and behavioral data for the degree 21 bird pairing

data_sources.name: Ansel Adams Archive — Center for Creative Photography
data_sources.description: The University of Arizona's Center for Creative Photography holds the Ansel Adams archive — over 2,500 fine prints, 40,000 negatives, and extensive correspondence. Adams was born February 20, 1902 — the same calendar date as Glenn's flight, 60 years apart.
data_sources.relevance: Dedication pairing — Adams showed America its landscape from new perspectives; Glenn showed America its planet from orbit
data_sources.license: Various (copyright held by The Ansel Adams Publishing Rights Trust)
